privacyPolicy.title: Privacy Policy
privacyPolicy.subtitle: Your privacy is important to us. This notice explains how we handle your information.
privacyPolicy.footerNote: By continuing to use Pregnancy Toolkits, you acknowledge that you have read and understood this Privacy Policy. For the full legal text, please contact us.

privacyFirst.title: Privacy-First Approach
privacyFirst.content: We offer all services without the collection of personal data. Our application is specialized in providing tools to support pregnant women, with strict adherence not to collect user data for any commercial purposes. All health data is stored locally on your device.

collection.title: Information We Collect
collection.desc: We collect personal information that you voluntarily provide to us when you express an interest in obtaining information about us or our products and Services.
collection.noSensitive: We do NOT process sensitive personal information
collection.noThirdParty: We do NOT collect information from third parties
collection.paymentSecure: Payment data is handled securely by Google Play

localStorage.title: Local Data Storage
localStorage.desc: All personal health data, appointments, and tracking information you enter into our tools is stored locally on your device only using browser localStorage/JSON.
localStorage.notShared: Not shared with any third parties
localStorage.canDelete: Can be deleted at any time

processing.title: How We Process Information
processing.desc: We process your information to provide, improve, and administer our Services, communicate with you, for security and fraud prevention, and to comply with law.
processing.legalBasis: We process your information only when we have a valid legal reason to do so under applicable law.

sharing.title: Information Sharing
sharing.desc: We may share information in specific situations and with specific third parties:
sharing.businessTransfers: Business Transfers: During mergers, sales, or acquisitions
sharing.noSold: We have NOT sold or shared any personal information to third parties for commercial purposes in the past 12 months.

aiProducts.title: AI-Powered Products
aiProducts.desc: We offer products powered by artificial intelligence through third-party service providers including Perplexity AI.
aiProducts.dataNote: When using AI features, only your specific query (anonymized) is processed. Your stored health data remains on your device.

notifications.title: Notification Sounds
notifications.desc: Our application uses audio notification sounds generated locally using the Web Audio API.
notifications.noAudioCollected: No audio data collected or transmitted

retention.title: Data Retention
retention.desc: We keep your information for as long as necessary to fulfill the purposes outlined in this Privacy Notice unless otherwise required by law. When we have no ongoing legitimate business need to process your personal information, we will either delete or anonymize such information.

security.title: Information Security
security.desc: We have implemented appropriate technical and organizational security measures designed to protect the security of any personal information we process.
security.note: However, no electronic transmission over the Internet can be guaranteed to be 100% secure.

minors.title: Information from Minors
minors.desc: We do not knowingly collect data from or market to children under 18 years of age. By using the Services, you represent that you are at least 18 or that you are the parent or guardian of such a minor and consent to such minor dependent's use of the Services.

rights.title: Your Privacy Rights
rights.desc: Depending on your location, you may have certain rights regarding your personal information:
rights.access: Right to access your personal data
rights.optOut: Right to opt out of data processing

international.title: International Users
international.desc: We comply with applicable data protection laws including GDPR (EU/UK) and regional privacy laws.
international.eu: EU/UK: GDPR compliance with consent-based processing
international.us: US: State-specific privacy rights (California, Virginia, etc.)
international.canada: Canada: Express and implied consent framework

updates.title: Policy Updates
updates.desc: We may update this Privacy Notice from time to time. The updated version will be indicated by an updated "Last updated" date. We encourage you to review this Privacy Notice frequently to be informed of how we are protecting your information.

contact.title: Contact Us
contact.desc: If you have questions or comments about this notice:

contactPage.title: Contact Us
contactPage.subtitle: Have questions or feedback? We'd love to hear from you.

dueDatePage.info: Only about 5% of babies are born on their exact due date. Most are born within 2 weeks before or after. Your healthcare provider may adjust your due date based on ultrasound measurements.

kickCounterPage.title: Kick Counter
kickCounterPage.subtitle: Count 10 kicks to monitor fetal activity
kickCounterPage.goal: Goal: 10 kicks in 2 hours or less
kickCounterPage.info: Starting at 28 weeks, count fetal movements daily. Contact your healthcare provider if you notice decreased movement or don't reach 10 kicks in 2 hours.

contractionPage.subtitle: Track duration and frequency of contractions
contractionPage.callProviderDesc: Contractions are 5 minutes apart and lasting 45+ seconds. Consider heading to the hospital.
contractionPage.rule511Desc: Call your provider when contractions are 5 minutes apart, lasting 1 minute each, for at least 1 hour.

diaperPage.subtitle: Track your baby's diaper changes
diaperPage.info: Tracking diaper changes helps monitor your baby's hydration and health. Newborns typically need 6-8 wet diapers daily.

symptomAnalyzer.title: AI Symptom Analyzer
symptomAnalyzer.subtitle: Educational insights for pregnancy symptoms
symptomAnalyzer.disclaimer: This tool provides educational information only. Always consult your healthcare provider for medical advice.
symptomAnalyzer.discussWithProvider: Discuss with your healthcare provider for personalized advice
symptomAnalyzer.remember: Remember: Every pregnancy is unique. These insights are for educational purposes only and should not replace professional medical advice.

nausea.early: Very common in the first trimester. Try small, frequent meals and ginger.
nausea.late: Less common at this stage. If severe or accompanied by other symptoms, consult your doctor.

fatigue.early: Normal for this stage. Your body is working hard! Prioritize rest.
fatigue.mid: You might be in the 'honeymoon phase', but listen to your body if you need rest.

headache.general: Hormonal changes and increased blood volume can cause headaches. Stay hydrated and rest in a dark room.

backpain.early: Monitor closely. If accompanied by cramping, contact your provider.
backpain.late: Common as baby grows and center of gravity shifts. Gentle stretching and good posture help.

cramps.general: Mild cramping can be normal as your uterus stretches. Severe or persistent cramping should be evaluated by your doctor.

swelling.general: Mild swelling in feet and ankles is common, especially later in pregnancy. Elevate your feet and stay hydrated. Sudden or severe swelling requires immediate medical attention.

heartburn.general: Very common during pregnancy due to hormonal changes. Eat smaller meals, avoid spicy foods, and don't lie down right after eating.

insomnia.general: Sleep difficulties are common. Try a consistent bedtime routine, use pillows for support, and limit screen time before bed.

moodswings.general: Hormonal fluctuations make mood changes normal. Talk to your support network and consider relaxation techniques.

dizziness.general: Can be caused by blood pressure changes. Stand up slowly, stay hydrated, and eat regular meals. If frequent, tell your doctor.

insights.default: This is a known pregnancy symptom. Monitor intensity and frequency and discuss with your provider.
insights.defaultFallback: Symptom noted. Discuss persistence with your doctor.

vitaminTracker.title: Vitamin Tracker
vitaminTracker.subtitle: Track your daily prenatal vitamins
vitaminTracker.tipContent: Take iron with vitamin C (orange juice) for better absorption, and avoid taking it with calcium or tea.

fitnessCoach.title: AI Fitness Coach
fitnessCoach.subtitle: Safe exercises customized for your pregnancy stage
fitnessCoach.disclaimer: Consult your healthcare provider before starting any exercise program.
fitnessCoach.greatJob: Great job! You completed your workout today!
fitnessCoach.fitnessVideosSubtitle: Safe workout guides for every trimester
fitnessCoach.safetyText: Stop immediately if you feel dizziness, pain, or shortness of breath. Keep water nearby.

exerciseDescs.prenatalSquatsDesc: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart. Lower gently as if sitting in a chair.
exerciseDescs.birdDogDesc: On hands and knees, extend opposite arm and leg. Hold for balance.
exerciseDescs.pelvicTiltsDesc: Gently rock your pelvis forward and back to relieve lower back pressure.
exerciseDescs.wallPushupsDesc: Stand arm-length from wall. Lean in and push back out.
exerciseDescs.butterflyStretchDesc: Sit with feet together, knees out. Gently lean forward.
exerciseDescs.seatedMarchingDesc: Sit on a stable chair or ball. Lift knees alternately.
exerciseDescs.neckRollsDesc: Slowly roll your head in gentle circles. Switch direction after 15 seconds.
exerciseDescs.armCirclesDesc: Extend arms to sides and make small circles. Gradually increase size.
exerciseDescs.gluteBridgeDesc: Lie on your back, knees bent. Lift hips towards the ceiling and hold.
exerciseDescs.sideLyingLegDesc: Lie on your side. Lift the top leg slowly and lower with control.
exerciseDescs.stepTouchDesc: Step side to side in a controlled rhythm. Keep your core engaged.
exerciseDescs.catCowDesc: On all fours, alternate between arching and rounding your back.
exerciseDescs.sideStretchDesc: Stand tall, reach one arm overhead and lean gently to the side.
exerciseDescs.childPoseDesc: Kneel and sit back on heels. Extend arms forward and rest forehead down.
exerciseDescs.deepBreathingDesc: Inhale deeply through nose for 4 counts, hold 4, exhale through mouth for 6.
exerciseDescs.ankleCirclesDesc: Lift one foot and rotate your ankle in slow circles, then switch direction.
exerciseDescs.hipCirclesDesc: Stand with feet apart and rotate your hips in wide circles.
exerciseDescs.shoulderShrugsDesc: Lift your shoulders towards your ears, then slowly lower them. Repeat.
exerciseDescs.wallSitDesc: Lean your back against a wall and slide down until knees are at 90 degrees. Hold.
exerciseDescs.calfRaisesDesc: Stand straight, raise your heels off the ground, hold, then lower slowly.
exerciseDescs.sumoSquatDesc: Stand with feet wider than shoulders, toes pointed out, and lower down.
exerciseDescs.standingMarchDesc: March in place, lifting knees high alternately while swinging arms.
exerciseDescs.lowImpactJacksDesc: Open arms and step one leg out at a time — a gentle version of jumping jacks.
exerciseDescs.kegelExerciseDesc: Squeeze your pelvic floor muscles as if stopping urination. Hold 5 seconds, release.
exerciseDescs.seatedTwistDesc: Sit upright and slowly twist your torso to the right, then left, while breathing.
exerciseDescs.hamstringStretchDesc: Sit and extend one leg forward, gently reaching for your toes.
exerciseDescs.figureFourDesc: Lie down, place one ankle on the opposite knee, and pull gently towards you.
exerciseDescs.savasanaDesc: Lie on your back or side, close your eyes, breathe deeply and relax completely.
exerciseDescs.gentleTwistDesc: Lie on your back, drop both knees gently to one side, then switch.

t1.title: First Trimester (Weeks 1-13)
t1.text: Focus on low-intensity exercises. Avoid overheating and stay well hydrated. Listen to your body and rest when needed.

t2.title: Second Trimester (Weeks 14-26)
t2.text: Your energy is higher! Great time for moderate exercise. Avoid lying flat on your back for long periods. Use support when needed.

t3.title: Third Trimester (Weeks 27-42)
t3.text: Focus on gentle stretching and breathing exercises. Avoid high-impact movements. Prioritize comfort and balance support.

warmup.title: Warm-Up Routine
warmup.step1: Start with gentle neck rolls and shoulder shrugs to release tension.
warmup.step2: Do arm circles, starting small and gradually increasing the range.
warmup.step3: March in place gently for 30 seconds to get blood flowing.
warmup.step4: Take 5 deep breaths, expanding your ribs fully.

cooldown.title: Cool-Down Routine
cooldown.step1: Walk slowly in place for 30 seconds to lower heart rate.
cooldown.step2: Do gentle neck and shoulder stretches, holding each for 15 seconds.
cooldown.step3: Practice deep belly breathing for 1 minute.
cooldown.step4: End with a gentle full-body stretch and positive affirmation.

tips.tip1: Drink at least 8 glasses of water on workout days. Hydration helps prevent muscle cramps.
tips.tip2: The best time to exercise is when you feel most energetic. Many find mid-morning works well.
tips.tip3: Kegel exercises are invisible but powerful! Practice throughout the day for pelvic floor strength.
tips.tip4: Walking is one of the safest exercises during pregnancy. Aim for 20-30 minutes daily.
tips.tip5: Proper breathing during exercise is key. Never hold your breath — exhale on exertion.

Stay here as long as you need

grounding54321.name: 5-4-3-2-1 Grounding
grounding54321.desc: A sensory technique to reduce anxiety

progressiveRelaxation.name: Progressive Muscle Relaxation
progressiveRelaxation.desc: Release tension from head to toe
steps: Start with your feet - tense for 5 seconds, release
Move to your calves - tense and release
Continue to thighs, abdomen, hands, arms
Tense and release shoulders, neck, face
Feel the wave of relaxation through your body

mobilityCoach.title: AI Mobility Coach
mobilityCoach.subtitle: Walking tips & leg cramp prevention for pregnancy
mobilityCoach.startWalkDesc: Start a gentle walk for better circulation
mobilityCoach.tip1: Walk for 20-30 minutes daily at a comfortable pace
mobilityCoach.tip2: Stay hydrated before, during, and after walks
mobilityCoach.tip4: Do calf stretches before bed to prevent night cramps
mobilityCoach.tip5: Keep magnesium-rich foods in your diet

calfRaises.name: Calf Raises
calfRaises.desc: Stand and raise heels off ground, lower slowly

ankleCircles.name: Ankle Circles
ankleCircles.desc: Rotate each ankle in circles, both directions

toePoints.name: Toe Points
toePoints.desc: Point and flex toes to improve circulation

postureCoach.title: AI Posture Coach
postureCoach.subtitle: Improve pregnancy posture to reduce back pain and discomfort
postureCoach.warning: Stop any exercise if you feel pain, dizziness, or discomfort. Consult your healthcare provider before starting any exercise routine.

massageGuide.subtitle: Safe massage techniques for pregnancy comfort

babySleep.noDataYet: Record at least 2-3 sleep sessions to get personalized AI advice.

sushi.name: Sushi (Raw Fish)
sushi.reason: Risk of parasites and bacteria like Listeria

coffee.name: Coffee
coffee.reason: Limit caffeine to 200mg/day to reduce risk of low birth weight

salmon.name: Salmon (Cooked)
salmon.reason: Great source of Omega-3s. Ensure it is fully cooked.

deliMeat.name: Deli Meat
deliMeat.reason: Listeria risk unless heated until steaming hot

tuna.name: Tuna (Albacore)
tuna.reason: Higher mercury content. Limit to 6oz per week.

alcohol.name: Alcohol
alcohol.reason: No known safe amount. Can cause fetal alcohol spectrum disorders.

hardCheese.name: Hard Cheeses
hardCheese.reason: Generally safe as they are pasteurized and have low moisture

chicken.name: Chicken
chicken.reason: Excellent protein source. Must be fully cooked.

gestationalDiabetes.title: GDM Risk Assessment
gestationalDiabetes.subtitle: Know your gestational diabetes risk factors

cycleTracker.title: Cycle & Ovulation Tracker
cycleTracker.subtitle: Track your cycle and find your fertile days
cycleTracker.trackTip: Track at least 3-6 cycles for more accurate predictions. Cycle length can vary due to stress, illness, or other factors.
cycleTracker.videosSubtitle: Learn about cycle tracking and reproductive health

weightGain.title: Weight Gain Analyzer
weightGain.subtitle: Monitor healthy pregnancy weight
weightGain.chartNote: Shaded area shows the recommended weight gain range

below.message: Your weight gain is below the recommended range.
below.recommendation: Consider consulting your healthcare provider about nutrition.

above.message: Your weight gain is above the recommended range.
above.recommendation: Discuss with your doctor about healthy eating habits.

normal.message: Your weight gain is within the healthy range!
normal.recommendation: Keep up the good work with balanced nutrition.

bloodType.title: Blood Type Calculator
bloodType.subtitle: Predict your baby's possible blood type
bloodType.rhWarning: When the mother is Rh-negative and father is Rh-positive, the baby may be Rh-positive. Your doctor may recommend RhoGAM injections during pregnancy to prevent complications.
bloodType.disclaimer: This calculator shows possible blood types based on genetics. The actual blood type will be confirmed through testing after birth. Always consult your healthcare provider for medical advice.

bmi.title: Pregnancy BMI Calculator
bmi.subtitle: Calculate your pre-pregnancy BMI and weight gain recommendations
bmi.disclaimer: These recommendations are based on Institute of Medicine guidelines. Your healthcare provider may give personalized advice based on your specific health situation and pregnancy.

fetalDevelopment.title: Baby Development Timeline
fetalDevelopment.subtitle: Week by week journey of your baby's growth

developments.week4: Cells are dividing rapidly. The neural tube is forming.
developments.week6: Heart begins to beat. Brain and spinal cord are developing.
developments.week8: Tiny fingers and toes are forming. Heart is beating steadily.
developments.week10: All vital organs have formed. Baby starts to move.
developments.week12: Reflexes are developing. Fingernails are forming.
developments.week14: Can make facial expressions. Gender may be visible.
developments.week16: Can make sucking motions. Bones are hardening.
developments.week18: You may feel first kicks! Ears are in final position.
developments.week20: Can hear sounds. Regular sleep cycles begin.
developments.week22: Eyebrows and eyelids are fully formed.
developments.week24: Lungs are developing. Responds to sounds and light.
developments.week26: Eyes begin to open. Brain is developing rapidly.
developments.week28: Eyes can open and close. Baby can dream!
developments.week30: Brain continues rapid growth. Can regulate body temperature.
developments.week32: Practicing breathing. Bones are fully formed but soft.
developments.week34: Fingernails reach fingertips. Fat layers developing.
developments.week36: Lungs are nearly mature. Head may engage in pelvis.
developments.week38: Fully developed and gaining weight. Ready soon!
developments.week40: Fully developed and ready for birth! Welcome to the world!

tips.week22: Baby can taste what you eat.

gdm.title: Gestational Diabetes Risk
gdm.subtitle: Assess your risk factors for gestational diabetes
gdm.disclaimer: This is an educational screening tool only. It does not diagnose gestational diabetes. Always consult your healthcare provider for proper testing and diagnosis.
gdm.infoNote: Gestational diabetes screening typically occurs between weeks 24-28. If you have multiple risk factors, your doctor may recommend earlier screening. This tool is for educational purposes only and does not replace medical advice.

high.title: Higher Risk
high.description: Your risk factors suggest elevated likelihood. Early screening may be recommended.

moderate.title: Moderate Risk
moderate.description: You have some risk factors. Standard screening at 24-28 weeks is recommended.

low.title: Lower Risk
low.description: You have few risk factors, but routine screening at 24-28 weeks is still recommended.

riskFactors.family: Family history of diabetes (parent or sibling)
riskFactors.previousLarge: Previously gave birth to a baby over 9 lbs

preventionTips.tip1: Maintain a healthy weight before and during pregnancy
preventionTips.tip2: Eat a balanced diet rich in fiber and whole grains
preventionTips.tip3: Stay physically active with doctor-approved exercises
preventionTips.tip4: Attend all prenatal appointments for regular monitoring
preventionTips.tip5: Limit sugary foods and refined carbohydrates

walkingCoach.title: Smart Walking Coach
walkingCoach.subtitle: Personalized walking program for a healthy pregnancy

walkingTips.tip4: Walk during cooler parts of the day

walkingCoach.warningMessage: Stop walking if you experience dizziness, shortness of breath, chest pain, contractions, or any unusual symptoms. Always consult your healthcare provider.

trimesterNotes.1: Start slowly, listen to your body

benefits.1: Sustained energy from fiber and healthy fats

benefits.4: Healthy fats for baby brain development
benefits.8: High protein for muscle and baby growth

recipes.2: Layer yogurt, granola, and berries. Drizzle with honey.

moderate.title: Moderate

high.title: Seek Support

q1.question: I have been able to laugh and see the funny side of things

movement.title: Gentle Movement

mealSuggestion.subtitle: Smart meal suggestions for your pregnancy

ovulation.getReady: Get ready for your fertile window!
ovulation.tipText: For the best chances, try to be intimate during your fertile window, especially 1-2 days before ovulation.

preeclampsiaRisk.title: Preeclampsia Risk Calculator
preeclampsiaRisk.subtitle: Evaluate your risk factors for preeclampsia
preeclampsiaRisk.riskFactorsDesc: Check all factors that apply to evaluate your risk level
preeclampsiaRisk.warningNote: If you experience any of these symptoms, contact your healthcare provider immediately.
preeclampsiaRisk.disclaimer: This tool identifies risk factors but cannot predict or diagnose preeclampsia. Regular prenatal care with blood pressure monitoring is essential for all pregnancies.

high.title: Higher Risk
high.description: You have one or more significant risk factors for preeclampsia. Your provider may recommend low-dose aspirin starting at 12 weeks.
high.recommendation: Discuss aspirin prophylaxis with your healthcare provider

moderate.title: Moderate Risk
moderate.description: You have some risk factors. Enhanced monitoring and lifestyle modifications may be recommended.
moderate.recommendation: Consider discussing risk with your healthcare provider

low.title: Lower Risk
low.description: You have few identified risk factors. Continue with standard prenatal care.

warningSigns.headaches: Severe headaches that don't go away
warningSigns.weightGain: Sudden weight gain (more than 5 lbs in a week)

t1.detail: Aim for 8-10 glasses of water daily to support amniotic fluid and reduce swelling.

t2.detail: Leafy greens like spinach and kale are packed with folate essential for neural development.

t3.detail: Walking improves circulation, reduces swelling, and helps with labor preparation.

t4.detail: Write down 3 things you're grateful for today. This reduces stress and improves mood.

t5.detail: Your baby can hear you! Reading or singing strengthens your bond.

t6.detail: This position improves blood flow to your baby and reduces pressure on organs.

t7.detail: Salmon, walnuts, and chia seeds support baby's brain development.

t8.detail: Pelvic floor exercises prepare you for labor and prevent incontinence.

t9.detail: Join a prenatal class or online community for support and shared experiences.

t10.detail: After week 28, track baby movements. You should feel 10 kicks in 2 hours.

t11.detail: Reduce swelling by putting your feet up for 15 minutes several times a day.

t12.detail: A protein-rich breakfast stabilizes blood sugar and reduces nausea.

t13.detail: Gentle stretching relieves back pain and prepares your body for labor.

t14.detail: Step away from screens for 30 minutes to reduce stress and eye strain.

t15.detail: Play calming music for baby - they'll recognize it after birth!

nauseaRelief.title: AI Nausea Relief Coach
nauseaRelief.subtitle: Personalized relief strategies for morning sickness
nauseaRelief.vomitingCheckbox: I'm experiencing vomiting, not just nausea
nauseaRelief.severeWarningDesc: If you can't keep any food/water down for 24 hours, see blood in vomit, or feel dizzy/faint, contact your healthcare provider immediately.
nauseaRelief.encouragingNote: Remember: Morning sickness usually peaks around weeks 8-10 and improves significantly by week 12-14. You've got this!
nauseaRelief.videosSubtitle: Techniques and tips to ease morning sickness

legCrampPreventer.subtitle: Prevent and manage pregnancy leg cramps with smart tips
legCrampPreventer.warning: Frequent or severe leg cramps, especially with swelling, redness, or warmth, should be reported to your healthcare provider immediately.

step2.title: Massage the muscle
step2.desc: Rub gently in the direction of the cramp

step3.title: Apply heat
step3.desc: Use a warm towel or heating pad

step4.title: Walk around
step4.desc: Gentle movement helps release the cramp

hydrated.title: Stay Hydrated
hydrated.desc: Drink at least 8-10 glasses of water daily

stretch.title: Stretch Before Bed
stretch.desc: Gentle calf stretches can prevent nighttime cramps

magnesium.title: Magnesium-Rich Foods
magnesium.desc: Eat nuts, seeds, bananas, and leafy greens

standing.title: Avoid Standing Too Long
standing.desc: Take breaks and elevate your legs when possible

sleep.title: Sleep Position
sleep.desc: Sleep on your left side with pillow between knees

toes.title: Avoid Pointing Toes
toes.desc: Flex feet instead of pointing when stretching

stretchReminder.title: Smart Stretch Reminder
stretchReminder.subtitle: Personalized stretching reminders to keep you comfortable

tips.tip2: Never force a stretch - go gently

neckRolls.name: Neck Rolls
neckRolls.desc: Gently roll your head in circles

shoulderShrugs.name: Shoulder Shrugs

sideStretch.name: Side Stretch
sideStretch.desc: Reach arm overhead and lean to side

hipCircles.name: Hip Circles
hipCircles.desc: Stand and rotate hips in circles

calfRaises.name: Calf Raises
calfRaises.desc: Rise up on toes, then lower

deepBreathing.name: Deep Breathing

benefits.fightsFatigue: Fights fatigue and provides sustained energy

benefits.brainDev: Supports baby brain development with Omega-3s
benefits.strengthensBones: Strengthens bones for you and baby

instructionsText.morningEnergyInstructions: Blend all ingredients until smooth. Serve immediately for best texture.
instructionsText.ironPowerInstructions: Blend fruits and beets first, then add chia seeds. Drink immediately for maximum iron absorption.
instructionsText.sleepBetterInstructions: Blend until creamy. Drink 1 hour before bed for best results.
instructionsText.nauseaReliefInstructions: Blend gently to keep it refreshing. Drink cold on an empty stomach.
instructionsText.brainBuilderInstructions: Blend until smooth. Consume in the morning to boost brain function.
instructionsText.calciumCrushInstructions: Blend yogurt and banana first, then add remaining ingredients. Great as a snack.

groceryList.title: Smart Grocery List
groceryList.subtitle: AI-powered shopping list for pregnancy nutrition

groceryList.allSuperfoodsInList: All superfoods are already in your list

snackPlanner.subtitle: Healthy pregnancy snacks for every craving
snackPlanner.letAIPick: Let us pick a healthy snack for you!

snackingTips.tip1: Aim for snacks combining protein + carbs for sustained energy
snackingTips.tip2: Keep portable snacks in your bag for unexpected hunger
snackingTips.tip3: Eat every 2-3 hours to maintain stable blood sugar
snackingTips.tip4: Stay hydrated - sometimes thirst feels like hunger

vitaminTracker.subtitle: Track your daily prenatal vitamins
vitaminTracker.tipContent: Take iron supplements with vitamin C for better absorption, but avoid taking them with calcium.

babyGear.title: Baby Gear Recommender
babyGear.subtitle: Essential items checklist for your new arrival
babyGear.disclaimer: This is a general guide. Your specific needs may vary. Consider borrowing or buying secondhand for items used briefly.

descriptions.carSeat: Required for bringing baby home from hospital
descriptions.diapers: Stock up on newborn and size 1
descriptions.bottles: Even breastfeeding moms often need bottles
descriptions.monitor: Peace of mind while baby sleeps
descriptions.breastPump: For breastfeeding moms returning to work

tips.carSeat: Look for one that clicks into a stroller. Check expiration dates.
tips.crib: Ensure it meets current safety standards. Firm mattress only.
tips.diapers: Don't buy too many newborn size - babies grow fast!
tips.bottles: Start with slow-flow nipples. Get 4-6 bottles initially.
tips.onesies: Look for front snaps or zippers for easy diaper changes.
tips.stroller: Consider one that accepts your car seat for easier transitions.
tips.monitor: Audio-only is often sufficient. Video adds convenience.
tips.breastPump: Insurance often covers this. Check before buying.
tips.swing: Some babies love them, others don't. Consider borrowing first.
tips.diaperBag: Look for one with changing pad and multiple compartments.
tips.bathtub: The sink works fine too for newborns.
tips.swaddles: Muslin ones are breathable and versatile.
